Resolved from ambiguous group (any_of_endpoint) by output+behavior comparison. Both submit-and-run an XQL query: old start_xql_query_platform posts /xql_queries/submit/ (CortexPlatformCore.py:4893) then polls /xql_queries/results/info/ (CortexPlatformCore.py:4758); new runXQLQuery posts xqlSubmitPath /xql_queries/submit/ (xql/commands.go:19) then reads xqlResultsInfoPath /xql_queries/results/info/ (xql/commands.go:20). Old outputs (CortexPlatformCore.yml:3393-3413: GenericXQLQuery.execution_id/status/error_details/query_url/number_of_results/results/query_limit_modified) are all present on the new GenericQueryResponse (xql/commands.go:83-95, context path GenericXQLQuery); the new command adds widget_name/widget_description/query_name/timeframe/view_def/graph_data.

Resolved from ambiguous group ('/get_data') by args+output comparison. Old core-list-exception-rules and new getExceptionRules both operate on exception rules with identical Core.ExceptionRules.* context; args align 1:1 except old-only retrieve_all vs new-only limit, with several old singular arg names mapped to new plural (id->ids, rule_name->rule_names, platform->platforms, user_email->user_emails, rule_type->rule_types); outputs are identical apart from two alert->issue renames (GENERATING_ALERT_ID->GENERATING_ISSUE_ID, TRIGGER_ALERTS->TRIGGER_ISSUES).

Resolved from ambiguous group ('/get_data') by args+output comparison. Same operation and identical Core.IssueRecommendations context; single required array arg issue_ids on both sides, and outputs are an exact 1:1 (23 fields, none added/removed). The new command changed the underlying endpoint: old wraps /get_data while new uses /xsoar/quickactions (plus /xsoar/playbooks/metadata).

Resolved from ambiguous group ('/get_data') by args+output comparison. Same operation and identical Core.Scripts context; old script_name -> new script_names, old supported_platforms split into new windows_supported/linux_supported/macos_supported, old pagination page_size/page_number -> new limit/offset, and new adds script_descriptions/created_by/is_high_risk. Core output fields (name/description/*_supported/script_uid) match; new adds modification_date/created_by/is_high_risk and drops old script_id/script_inputs and Core.ScriptsMetadata.returned_count/filtered_count.

Resolved from ambiguous group ('/get_data') by args+output comparison. Same operation and identical Core.Asset context; args are almost exact 1:1 with new adding filter_object. Eight core scalar outputs match (name, id[primary], provider, external_provider_id, first_observed, last_observed, realm, strong_id); the old command declared many nested/breakdown fields that are not declared as struct fields on the new command (listed under removed). The new command changed the underlying endpoint: old wraps /get_data while new uses /assets.

Resolved from ambiguous group ('/rbac/get_users') by args+output comparison. This is a 2-old-to-1-new collapse: getSystemUsers is already matched to core-list-system-users, and per user a separate matched object is recorded here for core-list-users. getSystemUsers with no filter equals core-list-users. Old core-list-users has no args; new adds emails/names/limit filters. Outputs are identical 8 Core.User.* fields with no changes.

Resolved from ambiguous group ('/scripts/run_script') by args+output comparison. This is a 2-old-to-1-new collapse: runScriptExecuteCommands is already matched to core-run-script-execute-commands, and per user a separate matched object is recorded here for core-execute-command. Core inputs overlap (endpoint_ids, timeout, is_raw_command, command_type, action_id[hidden]); old command(array) -> new commands, old incident_id -> new case_id, old polling_interval_in_seconds/polling_timeout_in_seconds -> new interval_in_seconds/timeout_in_seconds; old-only args removed on new: hide_polling_output/is_core/script_uid/parameters/command_separator. The context node changed from ScriptResult to ScriptRun: old emitted full per-endpoint Core.ScriptResult.results.* while new emits only a trigger summary Core.ScriptRun.action_id + endpoints_count (action_id already common; endpoints_count added).
